items.title: Leidos
bullets: Engineered an input data scoping system (React/TypeScript) for automatic, by-value data transfer between components, and adapted it within one day to meet revised specifications after a requirements miscommunication.
Refined the RSPack build integration and built a demonstration showcasing a full Webpack to RSPack replacement for a core project area.

Developed custom backend API endpoints and defined new data types (using .NET C# and a virtualized database architecture) to build core functionality for an internal monthly project status review application and a dynamic component input scoping system.

Led migration of a critical HR sync from SOAP to REST (PowerShell, C#) ahead of the vendor's January 1 SOAP sunset; inherited a broken integration, stabilized it, and delivered the working migration to the HR event teams.

Primary owner of the Cvent Active Directory sync, a nightly integration that reconciles 40,000 to 60,000 contact records against Active Directory to keep employee data current across enterprise systems.

Continued full-stack development on the internal project visualization portal as a full-time engineer, building out the project status review application as a complete custom visualization suite using the same PL/SQL, .NET C#, and React stack established during the internship.
Work entirely within Docker containers and dev containers across all software development, and manage local container environments using Kubernetes.
Building and maintaining an Azure DevOps pipeline for the Node.js SDK being developed for the EHS API integration.
Remediating Log4j vulnerabilities across legacy Java integration API repositories and contributing to the planning of their broader modernization effort.
On loan to the EHS team to help deliver a critical API integration; identified an opportunity to leverage the main team's internal API hosting platform as the solution, benefiting both teams.
Building a reusable Node.js SDK to bridge the EHS integration with the main team's API platform, while guiding a colleague building a parallel Python SDK.
Implemented authentication and authorization patterns (JWT/OAuth, RBAC) across internal API integrations and the Node.js SDK for the EHS API platform.
Designing a deterministic AI output system with hallucination markers that signal whether AI-generated content may be fabricated; currently demoing it internally.
Developed a Python proof-of-concept demonstrating how OData can enable AI to query structured databases, shared with upper management as part of broader AI capability exploration.
Advise the team's tech lead on AI architecture decisions and contribute hands-on MCP experience to internal AI tooling and integration workflows.
Built and maintain custom MCP servers locally, applying hands-on MCP architecture experience to internal AI tooling decisions at Leidos.

items.title: Leidos
bullets: Researched Module Federation for a microfrontend architecture, diagnosing Webpack compatibility issues and identifying RSPack as the only configuration that resolved Hot Module Replacement across remotes, leading to its adoption across the project.
Designed a database virtualization approach for flexible, user-creatable virtual tables within a rigid SQL structure; the design was adopted and built into the project using EF Core.

Built 12 data visualizations end-to-end across the full stack: custom PL/SQL for data retrieval, .NET C# API endpoints, and React frontend components, each tailored to the specific data shape and display requirements of the visualization. Implementations ranged from reusable table components to complex custom charts built on third-party libraries.
Built a dynamic form generation system that rendered custom form layouts from JSON schema definitions, enabling flexible UI configuration without code changes.
Designed and implemented an image upload component with drag-and-drop functionality, base64 encoding, database storage on the component record, and automatic resizing on retrieval.
Tested Docker containers as part of Module Federation research to validate microfrontend isolation and portability across environments.

items.title: Missouri Western State University
bullets: Led a team of three students to design and build a website and database for biological specimens collected during a school biology research project, using HTML, JavaScript, and CSS.
Presented the completed project at the 2022 Conference of Applied Learning in Higher Education.

items.title: Freelance
bullets: Commissioned for $500 to design and develop a Discord bot in Java that interfaced with Wynncraft's API to track player looting patterns across server worlds, deployed to a private user group.
Delivered to client satisfaction, leading to a follow-on commission inquiry.
Designed and developed additional plugins for Minecraft servers in Java.

items.title: local-memory-mcp
items.sub_title: Python, ChromaDB, MCP, Docker, SSE, OAuth, sentence-transformers
bullets: Designed and shipped a local-first personal RAG memory system for AI assistants, exposing MCP tools (store, search, update, delete, version chains) over both stdio and SSE transports.
Implemented versioned update chains with soft-delete, deprecation flags, and conflict logging so memory history is preserved rather than destructively overwritten.
Built heuristic reconciliation and warning-first write responses with structured self-heal hints, enabling AI agents to self-correct risky writes without human intervention.
Architected around an AIX (AI eXperience) philosophy: prioritizing text-first chunks with minimal metadata over rigid schemas, optimizing for practical LLM retrieval quality.
Shipped full Docker Compose support, optional bearer token and OAuth auth modes, health checks, and backup/restore; production-ready for self-hosted personal and team deployments.
Released as MIT open source with 4 stars; includes full documentation covering setup, architecture, integrations (ChatGPT + Claude Desktop), and a public roadmap.

items.title: Food Oasis
bullets: Served as tech lead on a 5-person team, taking point on architecture and technical decisions given my experience level relative to teammates.

Built a web application to combat food deserts in the Orlando area, allowing users to search for healthy recipes with allergy/dietary filters, view nearby grocery stores on an interactive map, and discover local farmers markets.
Gained practical experience leading a collaborative team project from design through implementation across a full TypeScript/JS stack.

items.title: Devoria
bullets: Founded and lead development of Devoria, a Minecraft MMORPG built in Java on the Paper server framework.
Designed and implemented a custom item and damage system using custom item stats.
Designed and developed a custom mob system using Model Engine.

items.title: Library Management System
bullets: Built a Java library management system with a GUI frontend and Java backend as a school project.

items.title: Wynncraft Command Block Content
bullets: Built command block puzzles and cutscenes for the 1.19 Silent Expanse update.
Fixed command block-related bugs and collaborated with Grian on a community video task.
